name: Player 2 joins waiting_setup game returns 200
why: Per spec v2.3, a valid join on a game in waiting_setup must return 200 so the client can advance to the placement phase. Uses requires game_created to guarantee an empty game exists before the join attempt.

name: Join with missing player_id returns 400
why: Spec v2.3 requires player_id in the join body. An empty body is malformed and the server must return 400 rather than silently attaching a player or returning 500.

name: Duplicate join by player already in game returns 400
why: Per spec v2.3, a player who has already joined cannot join again and the server must return 400, not 409. This is explicitly called out in the spec as the preferred status. Uses requires game_joined so player 1 is already attached when the duplicate request fires.

name: Valid fire by player 1 on empty cell returns 200 with miss
why: The autograder places player 2 ships only at (0,1), (2,3), and (4,0) on an 8x8 grid, so row 7 col 7 is guaranteed empty. Per spec v2.3 a valid fire must return 200 with result miss and a next_player_id field to advance turn order.

name: Valid fire by player 1 on known ship cell returns 200 with hit
why: The autograder places a player 2 ship at row 0 col 1, so when player 1 fires at that cell on their first turn the server must return 200 with result hit. Per spec v2.3, result must be exactly hit or miss, never sunk.

name: Fire out of turn by player 2 returns 403
why: The game starts on player 1's turn. Per spec v2.3, any fire attempt by the wrong player must return 403 forbidden, not 400. This is explicitly called out in the spec as the required status.

name: Move history on fresh playing game returns 200
why: Per spec v2.3, GET moves must return 200 even when no shots have been fired so clients can render an empty history without special-casing the start of the game. Uses requires game_playing to guarantee the game has transitioned past waiting_setup.
